experiment: DOMEX-RAND-S463 — F-RAND1 epsilon-greedy Gini retest with Monte Carlo simulation
predecessor: DOMEX-NK-S460 (successor note: F-RAND1 Gini retest at S463)
question: Does epsilon-greedy dispatch (eps=0.15) reduce dispatch Gini >=0.05 over 20 sessions, as F-RAND1 hypothesizes?
expect: F-RAND1 predicts Gini decrease >=0.05 from baseline 0.506 over 20 sessions at eps=0.15. Prior claim (L-1053 theory section of randomness_probe.py): 'Expected Gini reduction 0.04-0.08 over 20 sessions at eps=0.15.' I predict this is FALSE: 20 sessions is only +2.3% of 881 existing dispatches, so marginal redistribution cannot overcome accumulated inequality.
actual: FALSIFIED. Epsilon-greedy (eps=0.15) INCREASES Gini by +0.007 to +0.009 over 20 sessions across all simulation methods. The mechanism cannot achieve the target because: (1) 20 new sessions represent only 2.3% of 881 accumulated dispatches — insufficient marginal weight; (2) the greedy arm (85% of sessions) feeds the richest domain (nk-complexity, N=61), amplifying inequality; (3) even the deviation arm picks from already-visited domains (N=4-155), not unvisited ones (N=0). Even the THEORETICAL OPTIMUM (all 20 sessions allocated to minimize Gini) only achieves -0.034 — still short of the -0.05 target.
diff: Prediction that F-RAND1 would fail: CONFIRMED. The mechanism is structurally incapable of achieving the stated target at current scale (881 dispatches, 48 domains). F-RAND1 Gini criterion is FALSIFIED for epsilon-greedy dispatch.

monte_carlo_simulation.method: 1000-trial Monte Carlo, each trial simulates 20 sessions of epsilon-greedy dispatch using randomness_probe.py logic
monte_carlo_simulation.verdict: Zero trials out of 1000 achieved the F-RAND1 target. All trials INCREASED Gini.

exact_session_replay.method: Deterministic replay of randomness_probe.py epsilon-dispatch for seeds S463-S482 (20 consecutive sessions)

results.deviations: 2/20 (10%) — S470 to protocol-engineering, S474 to meta
results.greedy_sessions: 18/20 (90%) all went to nk-complexity (current UCB1 top)

greedy_only_eps_0.note: All 20 sessions to top domain — worst case

uniform_random.note: Best randomness scenario — still short of -0.05 target

targeted_bottom_half.note: Modified epsilon-greedy deviating only to bottom-half domains

all_20_to_bottom_5.note: Extreme: all sessions to 5 lowest-N domains — still insufficient

theoretical_optimum.note: Greedy min-Gini allocation of 20 sessions — STILL short of -0.05 target

sessions_needed_for_minus_0_05.method: Optimal min-Gini allocation (all sessions to lowest-N domain each round)
sessions_needed_for_minus_0_05.finding: Even with PERFECT allocation, 20 sessions cannot achieve -0.05 Gini reduction at current scale (881 existing dispatches). Need ~40 optimally-allocated sessions, or ~100+ epsilon-greedy sessions.

root_cause_analysis.primary: Marginal weight insufficient. 20 new sessions = 2.3% of 881 existing dispatches. The accumulated history dominates the Gini calculation regardless of how new sessions are distributed.
root_cause_analysis.secondary: Greedy arm reinforces top domain. At eps=0.15, 85% of sessions go to the UCB1-top domain (currently nk-complexity, N=61), which is already in the top-5. This INCREASES inequality.
root_cause_analysis.tertiary: Deviation arm picks from already-visited domains. The 32 active-frontier domains all have N>=1, so even random picks add to already-counted domains rather than addressing the 14 domains with N=0 (invisible/frontier-depleted).
root_cause_analysis.structural: The Gini is a cumulative metric over ALL historical dispatches. A mechanism affecting 20 future sessions cannot meaningfully move a metric dominated by 881 past sessions. F-RAND1 criterion was set without accounting for base-rate dilution.

yield_analysis.yield_preservation: 90% of sessions follow UCB1 greedy (target: >=85%). MEETS yield criterion.
yield_analysis.note: The yield criterion is easily met precisely because epsilon is low (0.15). But low epsilon also means minimal redistribution — the two goals are in tension.

conclusion: F-RAND1 Gini criterion FALSIFIED for epsilon-greedy dispatch at eps=0.15 over 20 sessions. The mechanism is structurally incapable of achieving Gini reduction >=0.05 because 20 sessions represent insufficient marginal weight against 881 accumulated dispatches. Monte Carlo (n=1000): 0/1000 trials achieved the target; all trials INCREASED Gini by +0.003 to +0.010. Even the theoretical optimum (perfect min-Gini allocation of 20 sessions) only achieves -0.034. The F-RAND1 target was set without accounting for base-rate dilution at scale. Recommendations: (1) Revise F-RAND1 criterion to realistic effect size (-0.02 per 20 sessions, or -0.05 per 50 sessions with optimal allocation); (2) If Gini reduction is genuinely desired, implement floor-allocation or round-robin for bottom-quartile domains (L-927 prescription) instead of epsilon-greedy; (3) Consider resetting Gini measurement window to rolling-20-session instead of cumulative-all-time, which would make epsilon-greedy effective.
effect_size: +0.007 (epsilon-greedy INCREASES Gini; direction opposite to hypothesis)
confidence: HIGH (Monte Carlo n=1000 trials, 0% success rate, theoretical optimum also insufficient)
